title: 10 best herbal creams for bleeding gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with bleeding gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and glycosides, work together to reduce swelling and infection in the gums. By promoting a healthy oral environment, Echinacea purpurea creams can help to reduce inflammation, ease pain, and promote the healing of damaged gum tissue, ultimately contributing to the overall health and well-being of the gums. This natural remedy provides a soothing solution for gum issues.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, which can contribute to bleeding.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids have antimicrobial properties that help combat the bacteria that cause gum infections and inflammation, thereby reducing bleeding.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation, prevent tissue damage, and promote healing in the gums, which can help alleviate bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the most commonly used part of Echinacea purpurea for making creams to address bleeding gums because they contain a high concentration of echinacoside, an alkaloid with anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Flowers are used in creams for bleeding gums due to their high content of alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting effects that can help reduce gum inflammation.

Leaves: Leaves are used in creams to address bleeding gums because they are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in gum healing.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 3 cups of dried echinacea purpurea flowers in 6 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl and discard the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the infusion and heat it.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it thickens and cools to body temperature.
Transfer the cream to a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with bleeding gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which help soothe and calm the gum tissue. The cream's moisturizing properties also promote healthy gum growth, reducing inflammation and pain associated with bleeding gums. Regular application of Aloe barbadensis cream can help alleviate gum inflammation and promote overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, thus alleviating bleeding.
Cinnamatic acid: This phenolic compound exhibits antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that can cause gum infections and bleeding.
Catechins: These flavonoid compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce oxidative stress, inflammation, and damage to gum tissues, thereby preventing bleeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera gel from the leaves is used to create creams for bleeding gums due to its anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and heal irritated gums.
Leaves: Aloe vera gel from the leaves is also used to create creams for bleeding gums due to its antimicrobial properties that help prevent infections and promote healing.
Leaves: Aloe vera gel from the leaves is used to create creams for bleeding gums due to its ability to stimulate collagen production, which helps to repair and regenerate gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and mix 100g of aloe barbadensis gel with 20g of distilled water in a stainless steel bowl.
Combine 10g of candelilla wax and 5g of beeswax in a separate stainless steel bowl.
Melt the wax mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Gradually add the wax mixture to the aloe barbadensis mixture while continuously stirring with a spatula.
Pour the blended mixture into sterile glass jars and allow to cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams helps with bleeding gums because they contain salvenic acid and tannins, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause gum infections, thereby promoting healing and reducing bleeding. Additionally, Salvia officinalis has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated tissues, which can help alleviate gum inflammation and discomfort. Its topical application can also help to reduce bleeding and promote a healthy gum environment.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, thereby reducing bleeding.

Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpene, carnosic acid exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which help protect the gums from oxidative damage and inflammation, leading to reduced bleeding.
Ursolic acid: An alkane triterpenoid, ursolic acid has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help combat gum infections and reduce inflammation, subsequently reducing bleeding gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and heal bleeding gums.
Stems: They are used for their antibacterial properties, which help prevent infections and promote healing of bleeding gums.
Roots: They are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with bleeding gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the morning when the dew is still present on the leaves.
Infuse 1 cup of the fresh leaves in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Combine 1 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture until the beeswax and shea butter are fully melted and the cream is smooth and creamy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with bleeding gums because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, work to reduce inflammation in the gums, promoting healing and soothing irritated tissue. This helps to improve blood flow to the gums, reducing bleeding and promoting a healthy gum environment. Regular application of Ginkgo biloba cream can also help to strengthen gum tissue, making it a popular natural remedy for bleeding gums.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help with bleeding gums by reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function, thereby reducing bleeding and promoting healing.
Bilobalide: Contributes to reduced bleeding gums by inhibiting platelet activation and aggregation, which can cause blood vessels to constrict and lead to bleeding.

Ginkgolides: Assist in treating bleeding gums by preventing platelet-activating factor (PAF) from causing inflammation and blood vessel constriction, allowing for reduced bleeding and improved gum health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are commonly used due to their rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Ginkgo biloba buds are used due to their potential in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in gum tissues.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used due to their ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer when the seeds are mature.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Add 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of vitamin e oil to the infused oil mixture.
Heat the mixture over low heat until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream has a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with bleeding gums because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active ingredient, tea tree oil, has been shown to reduce bacterial loads and promote healthy gum tissue. It also helps to reduce inflammation and swelling, which can contribute to bleeding gums. By applying a Melaleuca alternifolia cream to the affected area, individuals can experience relief from bleeding gums and promote overall oral health and wellness. Regular use may also help prevent future gum issues.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, thereby controlling bleeding.
Cineole: As a terpene, cineole exhibits antimicrobial properties that prevent the growth of bacteria and other pathogens responsible for gum infections, which can cause bleeding gums.
Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the gums, ultimately stopping bleeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are rich in compounds like eucalyptol and cineole, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and treat bleeding gums.
Barks: The barks of Melaleuca alternifolia are used in creams for their astringent properties, which help reduce bleeding and inflammation in the gums.
Stems: Stems of Melaleuca alternifolia are also used in creams for their ability to stimulate blood clotting and reduce inflammation in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Gently combine 10g of dried leaves in a muslin bag and add it to 500ml of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours while occasionally stirring to infuse the oil with herbal properties.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Add 5g of beeswax and 5g of shea butter to the oil mixture and heat it gently until the mixture is fully incorporated.
Pour the cream mixture into a stainless steel mold and allow it to cool and harden for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with bleeding gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The active compounds in Eucalyptus globulus, such as eucalyptol, help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the gums, promoting a healthy environment for healing. Regular application of these creams can also improve blood flow to the affected area, reducing bleeding and swelling, while also soothing the discomfort associated with gum inflammation, thereby aiding in the recovery of bleeding gums.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): It has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in bleeding gums.
Catechins (Phenolic compounds): They exhibit astringent properties, which help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding in gums.
Tannins (Phenolic compounds): They possess astringent properties that help to reduce inflammation, prevent bacterial growth, and promote healing in bleeding gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus leaves are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and healing gums.
Barks: Eucalyptus bark contains compounds like terpinen-4-ol, which has antimicrobial properties that help combat gum infections.
Oils from leaves: The essential oils extracted from Eucalyptus leaves have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, reducing swelling and preventing infection in gums.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of  coconut oil  and 250 grams of beeswax in separate containers.
Melt the coconut oil  in a double boiler at 180-200 degrees fahrenheit for 10-15 minutes.
Add 100 grams of eucalyptus globulus essential oil to the melted coconut oil mixture and stir well.
Combine the melted coconut oil mixture with the 250 grams of melted beeswax and stir until smooth.
Pour the mixture into containers and allow to cool and harden at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with bleeding gums because they contain quinine, a natural compound with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Quinine helps to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections that can cause bleeding gums. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis creams have a stimulating effect on the gums, promoting blood clotting and reducing bleeding. The antiseptic properties of quinine also help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections, promoting a healthy environment for gum healing.
remedy_constituents: Quinic acids: Help with bleeding gums by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, thus addressing the underlying causes of bleeding.
Alkaloids: Aid in preventing bleeding gums by reducing bacterial growth and infection, as well as by providing anti-inflammatory properties to soothe the gums.
Tannins: Help by contracting blood vessels and reducing bleeding, thus providing immediate relief from bleeding gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of quinine and other alkaloids that help reduce inflammation and prevent bleeding.
Barks: Used due to their rich source of quinine and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to treat gum infections.
Roots: Used due to their ability to stimulate blood clotting and reduce inflammation, making them effective in treating bleeding gums.
remedy_recipe: Extract 30 grams of cinchona officinalis bark and dry it in a low-temperature oven for 30 minutes at 150 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine the dried cinchona officinalis bark with 100 milliliters of vegetable glycerin in a saucepan and heat it over low heat.
Simmer the mixture for 2 hours then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax with 100 milliliters of coconut oil and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil in a saucepan.
Combine the strained cinchona officinalis mixture with the beeswax mixture and stir until it thickens into a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with bleeding gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream contains triterpenoid compounds that reduce swelling and fight off bacteria that cause infections, promoting healthy gum tissue and reducing inflammation. Additionally, calendula's antiseptic properties prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi, creating an environment that fosters healing and regeneration of gum tissue, ultimately alleviating bleeding gums and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Triterpene saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in bleeding gums, promoting a healthy environment for healing.
Phenolic acids (specifically ferulic acid): They possess antibacterial and antifungal properties, preventing infections that can worsen bleeding gums and promoting a clean wound site.
Naphthoquinones: These compounds exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, helping to protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals and reducing inflammation to promote faster healing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm bleeding gums.
Leaves: They are used due to their antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to bleeding gums.
Stems: They are used due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce swelling and promote healing in bleeding gums.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 cups of calendula officinalis flowers in 4 cups of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids after 2 hours.
Combine 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax and shea butter are fully incorporated.
Pour the cream into a clean container and let it cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with bleeding gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The licorice root extract in these creams has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums, making it easier to manage bleeding and discomfort. Additionally, its antiseptic properties help to prevent infection and promote a healthy oral environment, which is essential for stopping bleeding gums. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe and heal oral issues, providing a safe and effective solution for those suffering from bleeding gums.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, thereby alleviating the bleeding.
Liquiritigenin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the gums, reducing the severity of bleeding.
Isoflavans: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help to protect the gums from further damage and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps to reduce gum inflammation and bleeding.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and saponins, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, aiding in soothing and healing bleeding gums.
Barks: They are also a source of glycyrrhizin and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Extract 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin for 2-3 hours.
Filter the extract through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid.
Combine 200 grams of distilled water with the filtered extract in a beaker to dilute it.
Add 100 grams of emulsifying wax and 50 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until melted.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify for 30-40 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with bleeding gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums. This, in turn, promotes the healing of gum tissues and reduces the risk of bleeding. Additionally, Aloe vera's antibacterial properties help combat the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections and bleeding. Regular application of Aloe vera cream can lead to healthier, pain-free gums.
remedy_constituents: Glucomannans: These soluble fibers help to reduce inflammation and bleeding by promoting the formation of a protective barrier on the gum surface, thereby preventing further irritation and damage.
Phenolic acids: These antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help to combat bacterial growth and reduce inflammation in the gums, which can help to alleviate bleeding and promote healing.

Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to reduce swelling and fight off infections that may be causing the bleeding gums, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a high concentration of aloe vera gel, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to reduce gum inflammation and promote healing.
Gel from Leaves: It's the most widely used part due to its high content of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene and other antioxidants that help to protect and heal gums.
Exudate from Leaves: It's used for its potent anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce gum swelling and pain, and promote healing of bleeding gums.
remedy_recipe: Collect fresh aloe vera leaves and soak them in distilled water for 30 minutes to 1 hour to soften the gel.
Extract the aloe vera gel by scooping it out with a spoon from the softened leaves.
Mix 1/2 cup of the aloe vera gel with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax has fully melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove the mixture from the double boiler and let it cool and thicken at room temperature for 30-60 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for bleeding gums are topical remedies containing natural ingredients that help soothe and heal inflamed gums, reducing bleeding and discomfort. These creams typically combine various herbs, including Echinacea purpurea, Aloe barbadensis, Salvia officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Melaleuca alternifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Cinchona officinalis, Calendula officinalis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Aloe vera. If left untreated, bleeding gums can lead to painful chewing, difficulty eating, and social isolation, negatively impacting one's quality of life and overall well-being.
